kernel.runner.command.new.help: Start a new session and clear the current context.
kernel.runner.command.history.help: Show message counts for the current session.
kernel.runner.help.title: **Available Commands**
kernel.runner.unknown_command: Unknown command: {cmd}. Use /help to see available commands.
kernel.runner.history_summary: Current session: {user_turns} user turns, {total} messages total.
kernel.runner.schedule.not_found: Error: no task with id '{job_id}' found.
kernel.runner.pending_disambiguation.non: I couldn't determine which task to continue. Please switch focus first.
kernel.runner.pending_disambiguation.int: I couldn't determine which task to continue. Try one of these commands:
kernel.runner.approval_denied:
This approval was denied, so the current action will not continue.
If you still want to proceed, start a new request and approve the new approval when it appears.
kernel.executor.authorization.once: User approved this write for one execution.
kernel.rollback.unsupported: Rollback is not supported for this receipt.
kernel.rollback.error.dirty_repo: Rollback is unsupported for dirty repository prestate.
kernel.rollback.error.artifact_missing: Rollback artifact could not be loaded.
kernel.rollback.error.prestate_hash_mism: Prestate artifact hash does not match the stored content hash.
kernel.approval.blocked_message:
{summary}{detail_block}

Approval ID: `{approval_id}`. The current step will resume after you confirm.
kernel.approval.model_prompt: {summary} (approval ID: {approval_id}). Use `/task approve {approval_id}`, `/task approve-always-directory {approval_id}`, or reply with "approve once {approval_id}" / "always allow this directory {approval_id}".
kernel.approval.template.push.title: Confirm Push to Remote
kernel.approval.template.push.summary: The agent is about to push the current repository's local commits to a remote repository.
kernel.approval.template.push.detail: This updates remote code state and may affect collaborators or deployments; the original command is available in the details.
kernel.approval.template.delete.title: Confirm Delete Operation
kernel.approval.template.delete.summary: The agent is about to delete local files or directories.
kernel.approval.template.delete.detail: This action may be irreversible, so it is worth confirming the scope first; the original command is available in the details.
kernel.approval.template.command.title: Confirm Command Execution
kernel.approval.template.command.summary: The agent is about to run a command that changes the current environment.
kernel.approval.template.command.detail: This command has side effects and needs your confirmation before it continues; the original command is available in the details.
The agent is about to modify a sensitive file: `{path}`.
This may affect local configuration, credentials, or system behavior, so it needs your confirmation.
kernel.approval.template.outside_workspa: The agent is about to write to a file outside the workspace: `{path}`.
You can approve it just once, or always allow this directory for the current session.
kernel.approval.template.single_file.sum: The agent is about to modify 1 file: `{path}`.
kernel.approval.template.single_file.det: A change preview has been generated; execution will continue after you confirm.
kernel.approval.template.multi_file.summ: The agent is about to modify {count} files.
kernel.approval.template.multi_file.deta: This is a batch local change, so it is worth confirming the scope first.
kernel.approval.template.network.title: Confirm External System Change
kernel.approval.template.network.summary: The agent is about to call the external service `{host}` and change remote state.
The agent is about to call {count} external services and change remote state.
kernel.approval.template.network.detail: This affects external systems and needs explicit confirmation.
kernel.approval.template.packet.summary_: The agent is about to perform an action that needs confirmation.
kernel.approval.template.fallback.title: Confirm Continue
kernel.approval.template.fallback.summar: The agent is about to perform `{tool_name}`.
kernel.approval.template.fallback.detail: This action has risk level {risk} and requires your confirmation before it continues.
kernel.approval.scheduler.create.title: Confirm Scheduled Task Creation
kernel.approval.scheduler.create.summary: The agent is about to create the scheduled task `{name}`. It will run {timing}.
kernel.approval.scheduler.create.detail: After you confirm, Hermit will start this task automatically on the configured schedule.
kernel.approval.scheduler.create.reason: This will let Hermit run automatically in the future, so you should confirm both the trigger timing and the task contents first.
kernel.approval.scheduler.update.title: Confirm Scheduled Task Update
kernel.approval.scheduler.update.summary: The agent is about to update scheduled task `{job_id}`.
kernel.approval.scheduler.update.detail: After you confirm, future automatic runs will use the new configuration.
kernel.approval.scheduler.update.reason: This changes how future automatic runs behave, so it is worth confirming the updated configuration first.
kernel.approval.scheduler.delete.title: Confirm Scheduled Task Deletion
kernel.approval.scheduler.delete.summary: The agent is about to delete scheduled task `{job_id}`.
kernel.approval.scheduler.delete.detail: After you confirm, this task will stop running automatically.
kernel.approval.scheduler.delete.reason: Deleting a scheduled task stops future automatic runs, so it needs an explicit confirmation first.
kernel.approval.scheduler.delete.effect: After you confirm, this task will immediately stop all future automatic runs.
kernel.approval.scheduler.item.name: Task name: `{name}`
kernel.approval.scheduler.timing.cron_wi: first around {next_run}, then repeated by cron `{cron_expr}`
kernel.approval.scheduler.timing.unknown: according to the saved schedule configuration
kernel.provider.approval_formatter.syste: You rewrite approval prompts into user-friendly English product copy. You must only use the supplied JSON facts and must not invent any targets, commands, risks, or services. Return strict JSON with exactly these keys: title, summary, detail. Keep summary and detail concise, clear, and human. Explain what the tool is about to do and why approval is needed. Do not dump raw shell commands into summary or detail unless absolutely necessary.
kernel.provider.available_commands.intro: The following slash commands are handled by the system layer (not the LLM), and users can invoke them directly. If the user asks what commands are available, tell them:
kernel.compact.system_prompt: You are a professional conversation summarization assistant. Compress the following conversation history into a concise summary that preserves key information, completed work, important decisions, and outstanding tasks. The summary should be detailed enough for a new conversation to continue seamlessly. Output only the summary body, with no extra explanation or heading.
kernel.compact.summary_missing: The LLM did not return a summary, so compaction was cancelled.
kernel.compact.assistant_ack: Understood. I have the previous conversation context and can continue.
kernel.compact.auto_notice: [System] Context was auto-compacted (last input {last_input_tokens:,} tokens exceeded the threshold {threshold:,}).
kernel.compact.command.help: Compact the current session context (LLM summary).
kernel.planner.mode.prompt:
<plan_mode>
You are currently in planning mode. Read-only tools (search, reading files, and similar) remain available so you can gather the information needed to make a plan; side-effecting tools (writing files, running commands, creating schedules, and similar) are disabled.
Please output a structured execution plan in Markdown with:
1. Task overview
2. Step-by-step plan (each step should explain the action and why)
3. Risks and watchouts
You may investigate with read-only tools before producing the final plan, but do not perform any side-effecting actions.
</plan_mode>
kernel.planner.execute_confirmed_prompt:
<execution_plan>
{plan_content}
</execution_plan>

The user confirmed execution. Follow the plan strictly, execute it step by step, and briefly report each completed step before continuing.
kernel.planner.execution_prompt:
<execution_plan>
{plan_content}
</execution_plan>

Follow the plan strictly, execute it step by step, and briefly report each completed step before continuing.
kernel.planner.closed: Plan mode is off. All tools are available again.
kernel.planner.confirm_missing_plan: There is no saved plan to execute. Generate one in plan mode first, then run /plan confirm.
kernel.planner.entered:
Entered plan mode. Read-only tools (search, file reads, and similar) are still available, and side-effecting actions are disabled.
Plans will be saved to {plans_hint}

Send your task and I will research it, then produce a structured plan without making any write-side changes.
After the plan is ready, say "start executing" or run /plan confirm to begin. Use /plan off to exit plan mode.
kernel.planner.command.help: Enter or exit plan mode; /plan off exits; /plan confirm executes the plan.
kernel.usage.title: **Current Session Token Usage**
kernel.usage.command.help: Show token usage for the current session.
kernel.runtime.error.resume_requires_too: Task resume requires a configured ToolExecutor
kernel.runtime.error.tool_use_without_bl: Provider requested tool_use without tool blocks.
kernel.runtime.error.tool_execution_requ: Task-scoped kernel executor is required for tool execution.
kernel.runtime.error.streaming_tool_exec: Task-scoped kernel executor is required for streaming tool execution.
kernel.runtime.error.tool_execution_miss: Tool '{tool_name}' requires task-scoped governed execution; task context is missing.
kernel.executor.error.snapshot_too_large: Runtime snapshot exceeds working-state size limit
kernel.executor.error.resume_messages_no: Runtime resume messages artifact is not a list

kernel.memory.retrieval_intro: Below are the most relevant cross-session memories; follow them only when relevant:
kernel.memory.static_intro: Below are cross-session memories; follow the long-term conventions in them:
kernel.nlp.signal.preference: from now on|always use|always reply|preference|habit|please always|never again|must|have to
kernel.nlp.signal.task_state: current|currently|todo|incomplete|completed|deleted|cleaned|in progress|has been set|user wants|needs rewrite|next step|later
kernel.nlp.signal.project_convention: default|convention|standard|naming|branch strategy|deployment process|unified in|commit message|working directory
kernel.nlp.continuation.corrective: I mean|I meant|that's not what I meant|you misunderstood|you got it wrong
